summary: Epic Dark Star > Other One > Lovelight suite with tight acoustic set
blurb: The show delivers a soaring Dark Star that segues into an expansive The Other One and a fiery Turn On Your Love Light. Jerry’s tone is on fire, Phil provides lead‑style bass, and the acoustic first half flows smoothly into a powerful electric second set. Recording quality is pristine soundboard capture.
review: On February 13, 1970 at the Fillmore East, the Dead turned in one of their early‑era masterpieces. The evening opens with a bright acoustic set—China Cat Sunflower into I Know You Rider, Me And My Uncle and Dire Wolf—setting up a relaxed yet tight vibe that carries through Smokestack Lightning and Uncle John's Band.  When the electric side erupts, Jerry Garcia launches an epic Dark Star whose soaring tone drives a seamless transition into a marathon The Other One, culminating in a blistering Turn On Your Love Light. Phil Lesh’s bass is practically a second lead guitar, while Bob Weir fuels The Other One with high‑energy rhythm and vocals.  Pigpen’s organ swells under the jam, and both Mickey Hart and Bill Kreutzmann carve out a memorable drum break during Lovelight.  Across all six known sources the performance is described as “legendary,” “all‑time great,” and “life‑changing.”  The Miller soundboard capture (gd1970-02-13.sbd.miller.97613.sbeok.flac16) offers crystal‑clear, pristine audio that lets every nuance of this historic night shine.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: All six recordings are marked positive; multiple reviews repeatedly praise the Dark Star/Other One/Lovelight suite, Jerry’s soaring tone, and the tight band interplay. Consistent high‑praise across soundboard, matrix, and audience sources warrants a strong positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Pristine soundboard capture with crystal‑clear detail, no dropouts, and the most celebrated rendition of the Dark Star > Other One > Lovelight sequence.

key_highlights: Epic Dark Star → The Other One → Turn On Your Love Light suite spanning over 20 minutes.
Tight acoustic first half that flows naturally into a powerful electric second set.
Jerry Garcia’s soaring tone and inventive lead work on Dark Star; Phil Lesh’s lead‑style bass shines throughout.
Excellent soundboard recording quality—pristine, crystal‑clear, and album ready.

band_performance.Jerry: Soaring tone on Dark Star and inventive lead throughout; described as being in his prime.
band_performance.Phil: Lead‑style bass that rides alongside Jerry, especially prominent in the electric set.
band_performance.Bob: Strong rhythm guitar and vocal work in the acoustic portion; drives The Other One with high energy.
band_performance.Mickey: Noted for a memorable drum break during Lovelight, adding dynamic texture.
band_performance.Billy: Creates an other‑worldly texture on drums during The Other One, enhancing the jam.
